prompt.customInstructions: eBay search: GET https://www.ebay.com/sch/i.html?_nkw=<query>. Server-rendered HTML; no auth. Each result is a <li class="s-card"> inside <ul class="srp-results">. REQUIRES browser mode: eBay's edge fingerprints TLS/HTTP2 and returns a "Pardon Our Interruption" splash (~13 KB, zero s-card elements) for plain Node/undici fetch, even with a forged Chrome User-Agent and full sec-ch-ua / sec-fetch-* headers. Real Chrome from Playwright passes. Response contains promo/placement cards with title "Shop on eBay" that MUST be filtered out. An .srp-save-null-search banner signals "No exact matches found" and any remaining cards are suggestions, not matches — return []. Card titles embed a hidden a11y span "Opens in a new window or tab" that should be stripped. Anchor hrefs are wrapped in tracking redirects; reconstruct a clean product URL from the <li data-listingid="..."> attribute as https://www.ebay.com/itm/<listingId>.
prompt.knownIssues: Akamai bot-manager: eBay is protected by Akamai, which does TLS/HTTP2 fingerprinting + JS-heuristic scoring. A plain Node/undici fetch returns the "Pardon Our Interruption" splash (~13 KB, zero s-card elements) regardless of User-Agent. Headless Chromium (Playwright) also gets redirected to /splashui/challenge once the IP is flagged — most commonly after a burst of requests from a datacenter egress. Mitigations already wired into this adapter: page.addInitScript stealth shim (hides navigator.webdriver, fills plugins/languages, stubs window.chrome and Permissions API), Sec-Fetch-* headers, a homepage warm-up + 1.5s idle before the SRP request, and an explicit /splashui/challenge detector that returns [] with a WARN log instead of blowing up. For steady-state production, route the site through a residential proxy (the site is already bound to the isp-usa pool — populate it in admin → Proxies, or set a per-site overrideUrl). Currency depends on visitor geolocation/cookie (USD by default for a cookieless US fetch); parse the rendered symbol (US $, AU $, C $, €, £, ¥, …) and map to ISO 4217. Prices can be ranges like "$10.00 to $20.00" — take the first numeric amount. Brand is not reliably extractable from the title; default to "unknown". The first 1-2 cards on most result pages are sponsored placements whose title is literally "Shop on eBay"; filter them by text, not by position.
prompt.apiNotes: eBay has a real Browse API (api.ebay.com/buy/browse/v1) requiring an OAuth client_credentials token. Not wired in; scraping the SRP via browser mode is sufficient for price/title/image and matches what ebay.com shows.
